title: Transitional Housing/Shelter * Homeless Youth - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Transitional Housing/Shelter * Homeless Youth 7 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER THE LOFT LIVING OPTIONS FOR TEENS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides both short term and long term shelter options for youth. Services include housing focused case management, education planning, job and employment search assistance, life skills training, and other supports in transitioning to self-sufficiency. The shelter opens as an emergency shelter as space allows. Eligibility: Ages 14-24 who are at risk of homelessness or experiencing housing insecurity Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (800) 660-0934 Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY WORKS TRANSITIONAL LIVING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a two year transitional living program. Homes are located throughout Jackson County. Offers comprehensive case management, life skills classes, support in finding employment, and awareness of educational opportunities. 2594 E Barnett Road Suite C Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Individuals age 18-23 who are homeless or at risk for homelessness Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 779-4357 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES NEXT STEPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a transitional living program with 25 long-term, apartment-style units. Offers supportive services, including case management and life skills training for independent living. The program has three tiers with placement determined by age, skills, needs, and availability. The location is determined after the application is approved. No emergency services. 225 SW 4th Street Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness or at risk of being homeless Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days a week Email (541) 360-8482 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH NEW AVENUES COLUMBIA STREET TRANSITIONAL HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional housing and help with finding apartments, getting vouchers and subsidies, and connecting to other services. Housing options include The Moxie, a 24-bed facility in downtown Portland; Unity House, for LGBTQ2+; New Doors, for youth transitioning from foster care; New Meadows, a 15-unit community for youth formerly in foster care; and Butterfly House, for youth involved in the sex trade. 1220 SW Columbia Street Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Varies by program; age 25 and under experiencing homelessness or housing instability, with a focus on former foster youth, individuals with disabilities, and LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Not applicable Email (503) 517-3912 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN YOUTH HOUSING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides on-site and community-supported housing, case management, and other support services. A few transitional housing units may be available for the homeless, parenting youth. Transitional housing is from 6-24 months. Parents with children are accepted for housing case management and transitional housing services and must participate in parent support case management. 1132 SW 13th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: -Homeless youth ages 18-24 -Some units available for parents age 18-24 with children under age 4 -Services are not for individuals passing through on travel Hours: Access Center: 7 days per week 8am-6pm Day Program Drop-In: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 9am-10am,1pm-2pm and 5pm-6pm, Tuesday/Thursday 9am-10am and 1pm-6pm, Sunday 1pm-6pm Email (503) 535-3800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITIONAL YOUTH YOUTH TRANSITIONAL HOUSING PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers transitional housing that provides structure and skills training that will help with transitioning into healthy self-sufficiency upon program graduation. Eligibility: Homeless and at-risk youth ages 18-24 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 350-7215 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP YOUTH OUTREACH SERVICES YOUTH TRANSITIONAL LIVING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides group apartment living. Clients will have access to employment services, education services, and life skills classes. 719 E 1st Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Ages 16-21 who are experiencing homelessness Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm (503) 538-8023 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY WORKS TRANSITIONAL LIVING PROGRAM JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES NEXT STEPS NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH TRANSITIONAL HOUSING OUTSIDE IN YOUTH HOUSING PROGRAM YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP YOUTH TRANSITIONAL LIVING PROGRAM
